title: Publish deterministic JSON test vectors for cross-implementation conformance

artifacts.description: Index of all test vectors, naming conventions, and how to run the conformance suite
JSON Schema for the test vector envelope: inputs, intermediates, expected_outcome, error_code
Happy-path: valid HumanUniqueness + AgentPolicy proofs, fresh nonce, pass
Agent credential expiry in the past, expect fail CREDENTIAL_EXPIRED
HumanUniqueness proof against an old root not in the on-chain window, expect fail STALE_ROOT
Delegation requests permission bits not present in parent credential, expect fail SCOPE_EXPANSION
Permissions byte has bit 4 set (FINANCIAL_UNLIMITED) without bit 3 (FINANCIAL_MEDIUM), expect fail INVALID_CUMULATIVE_ENCODING
Permissions byte has bit 3 set (FINANCIAL_MEDIUM) without bit 2 (FINANCIAL_SMALL), expect fail INVALID_CUMULATIVE_ENCODING
Delegation chain at max allowed depth (e.g. 3 hops), expect pass; one beyond max, expect fail DELEGATION_DEPTH_EXCEEDED
Same nullifierHash submitted twice, second attempt must fail NULLIFIER_ALREADY_USED
Delegation narrows READ_DATA+WRITE_DATA to READ_DATA only, expect pass
sessionNonce in human proof does not match agent proof binding, expect fail NONCE_MISMATCH
EdDSA signature on agent credential corrupted (single bit flip), expect fail INVALID_CREDENTIAL_SIGNATURE
Merkle inclusion path is for a different leaf, witness fails, expect fail INVALID_PROOF
Permissions byte is 0x00, agent credential with no bits set, expect fail per policy (NO_PERMISSIONS)
Permissions byte 0xFF with all cumulative rules satisfied, expect pass
SIGN_ON_BEHALF (bit 5) with valid cumulative state, delegation narrows it out, expect fail on subsequent use
SUB_DELEGATE (bit 6) present, chain produces a second-level delegation, expect pass
ACCESS_PII (bit 7) present in credential but stripped by delegation, downstream verifier rejects PII access
Checks that commitment = Poseidon(secret, nullifier_trapdoor) matches the expected hex; validates hash function alignment across implementations
Validates nullifierHash = Poseidon(nullifierTrapdoor, externalNullifier) matches expected hex
AgentPolicy commitment = Poseidon(modelHash, permByte, expiry, operatorPubKey) matches expected hex
Delegation circuit scope hash intermediate matches expected, validates hash chaining across implementations
Same as tv-001 but using PLONK proving system for AgentPolicy, validates both proof systems
Delegation proof via PLONK, expect pass, validates dual-build circuit parity
Permissions = 0b00000100 (bit 2 only), valid minimal financial credential, expect pass
Permissions = 0b00001100 (bits 2+3), valid cumulative encoding, expect pass
Permissions = 0b00011100 (bits 2+3+4), valid, expect pass
Permissions = 0b00000011 (bits 0+1), basic read+write, expect pass
Single-hop delegation (depth 1), valid, expect pass; intermediate commitment hash included
Two-hop delegation chain, valid, expect pass
Three-hop delegation at max depth boundary, expect pass; four-hop variant included as negative case
AgentPolicy proof uses a different operator public key than the one in the on-chain registry, expect fail OPERATOR_MISMATCH
Model hash in credential does not match the declared model, expect fail MODEL_HASH_MISMATCH
Node.js runner: loads all tv-*.json, calls @bolyra/sdk verify functions, asserts expected_outcome matches actual, outputs TAP format
Deterministic generator: uses fixed seed (documented in README) to produce all test vector JSON files via snarkjs witness generation + Poseidon hashing
Append Appendix A referencing spec/test-vectors/ with table of vector IDs, categories, and expected outcomes

steps: Define the JSON envelope schema (schema.json): fields = {id, title, category, inputs, intermediates, expected_outcome, error_code, proving_system}
Document the canonical fixed secret/key pair in README.md (deterministic BabyJubJub operator keypair, fixed human secret, fixed nullifier trapdoor, fixed externalNullifier)
Implement generate-vectors.js: use snarkjs + circomlibjs Poseidon to compute all intermediate hashes from fixed inputs and write each tv-*.json file deterministically
Generate tv-001 through tv-009 (core happy-path and primary failure modes) first, validate outputs manually against sdk verification functions
Generate tv-010 through tv-021 (nonce, signature, Merkle, intermediate hash alignment vectors)
Generate tv-022 through tv-023 (PLONK proving system parity vectors)
Generate tv-024 through tv-030 (cumulative bit encoding suite + delegation depth suite)
Generate tv-031 through tv-032 (operator mismatch, model hash mismatch)
Implement run-conformance.js TAP runner: import @bolyra/sdk, iterate all tv-*.json, invoke verifyHandshake or relevant verify primitive, assert outcome and error_code
Run conformance suite against current @bolyra/sdk v0.2.0, fix any discrepancies found (spec vs implementation drift)
Add conformance suite to package.json scripts as `test:conformance` and wire into CI alongside test:circuits:fast
Append Appendix A to spec/draft-bolyra-mutual-zkp-auth-01.md with a normative table of all vector IDs, their category, and expected outcome
Validate that all 30+ vectors are self-consistent: re-run generate-vectors.js with same seed produces byte-identical outputs
dependencies: circomlibjs (Poseidon hash function — must match circuit implementation exactly)
snarkjs (witness generation for intermediate value extraction)
circuits/build/ artifacts (.zkey, .vkey for Groth16 and PLONK AgentPolicy + Delegation)
validateCumulativeBitEncoding() from sdk (reference implementation for bit encoding rules)
Fixed BabyJubJub keypair documented and committed to README (never rotated — these are test-only keys)
